task_completion.description: Open Fiji and the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image
task_completion.visual_evidence: Fiji (ImageJ) application is open, and an image window titled 'blobs.gif' or 'Blobs' displaying black blob-like shapes on a white background is visible.

task_completion.description: Select and configure a counting tool for multiple categories
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Multi-point tool is selected in the Fiji toolbar, or the Cell Counter plugin window is open showing at least two distinct counter types/categories initialized.
At least 5 clearly circular/round blobs in the image have markers (dots, crosses, or numbers) of a specific color or type placed on them.
At least 5 clearly elongated, oval, or irregular blobs have markers of a DIFFERENT color or type placed on them, distinguishing them from the round objects.

task_completion.description: Generate or display the results table with coordinates and types
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Results' window or similar data table is visible in Fiji, containing rows for the marked points with columns for X, Y coordinates and a Counter/Type/Class identifier.

task_completion.description: Export the results to the specified CSV file path
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box is visible saving the table to '~/ImageJ_Data/results/manual_counts.csv', or a terminal/file manager confirms the creation of 'manual_counts.csv' in '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/'.

integrity.description: The agent used Fiji's GUI to manually mark the image
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent actively using the mouse/cursor to place markers on the image window in Fiji, rather than running a script in the terminal to bypass manual counting.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely classified the objects based on morphology
integrity.visual_evidence: The markers for the 'Round' category should actually be on round blobs, and 'Elongated' markers on elongated blobs. The agent should not be randomly clicking the same spot or ignoring object shapes.

integrity.description: The CSV file was exported from Fiji's results, not manually fabricated
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should use Fiji's 'File > Save As' or 'Results > Save' functionality. There should be no evidence of the agent opening a text editor (like nano or gedit) to manually type out fake coordinates and classes.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires using the 'Blobs (25K)' sample image in Fiji. The agent must mark at least 5 round objects (high circularity, > 0.85) and at least 5 elongated objects (low circularity, <= 0.80) using distinct counters. The final output must be saved exactly to '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/manual_counts.csv'.
